pattern: Each character contributes a value determined purely by its distance from 'z' in the alphabet times its 1-indexed position, so the whole answer is a single weighted-sum pass over the string.
hints: Figure out, for a single lowercase letter, what number it contributes when the alphabet is counted backwards from 'z' = 1 up to 'a' = 26.
Multiply that per-letter value by the letter's 1-indexed position in the string (the first character is position 1, not 0).
Sum those products across the whole string in a single left-to-right pass; no character's contribution depends on any other character.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, an alternating repeated pattern
single character at the low end of the reversed alphabet
single character at the high end of the reversed alphabet
every character identical, so position alone drives the growth
descending-then-ascending pair; a solution that reads the alphabet forward instead of reversed would get this wrong
the two alphabet extremes adjacent to each other
a longer word mixing many distinct letters

stress.description: random lowercase strings up to the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(1000, size))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
